33	
34	    private var loadJob: Job? = null
35	
36	    init {
37	        loadUsers()
38	    }
39	
40	    private fun loadUsers() {
41	        loadJob?.cancel()
42	        loadJob = viewModelScope.launch {
43	            manageUserUseCase.getAllActiveUsers()
44	                .onStart {
45	                    _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = true, errorMessage = null) }
46	                }
47	                .catch { e ->
48	                    _uiState.update {
49	                        it.copy(
50	                            isLoading = false,
51	                            errorMessage = e.message ?: "Failed to load users"
52	                        )
53	                    }
54	                }
55	                .collect { users ->
56	                    _uiState.update { state ->
57	                        state.copy(
58	                            users = users,
59	                            filteredUsers = filterUsers(users, state.searchQuery),
60	                            isLoading = false,
61	                            errorMessage = null
62	                        )
63	                    }
64	                }
65	        }
66	    }
67	
68	    fun onSearchQueryChanged(query: String) {
69	        _uiState.update { state ->
70	            state.copy(
71	                searchQuery = query,
72	                filteredUsers = filterUsers(state.users, query)
73	            )
74	        }
75	    }
76	
77	    fun clearError() {
78	        _uiState.update { it.copy(errorMessage = null) }
79	    }
80	
81	    private fun filterUsers(users: List<User>, query: String): List<User> {
82	        if (query.isBlank()) return users
83	        val lowerQuery = query.lowercase()
84	        return users.filter { user ->
85	            user.displayName.lowercase().contains(lowerQuery) ||
86	                user.username.lowercase().contains(lowerQuery)
87	        }
88	    }
89	}

15	data class PolicyListUiState(
16	    val policies: List<Policy> = emptyList(),
17	    val filteredPolicies: List<Policy> = emptyList(),
18	    val selectedType: PolicyType? = null,
19	    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
20	    val errorMessage: String? = null
21	)
22	
23	@HiltViewModel
24	class PolicyListViewModel @Inject constructor(
25	    private val managePolicyUseCase: ManagePolicyUseCase
26	) : ViewModel() {
27	
28	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(PolicyListUiState())
29	    val uiState: StateFlow<PolicyListUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
30	
31	    init {
32	        loadPolicies()
33	    }
34	
35	    private fun loadPolicies() {
36	        viewModelScope.launch {
37	            managePolicyUseCase.getAllActivePolicies().collect { policies ->
38	                _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
39	                    policies = policies,
40	                    filteredPolicies = applyFilter(policies, _uiState.value.selectedType),
41	                    isLoading = false
42	                )
43	            }
44	        }
45	    }
46	
47	    fun filterByType(type: PolicyType?) {
48	        val current = _uiState.value
49	        _uiState.value = current.copy(
50	            selectedType = type,
51	            filteredPolicies = applyFilter(current.policies, type)
52	        )
53	    }
54	
55	    private fun applyFilter(policies: List<Policy>, type: PolicyType?): List<Policy> {
56	        return if (type == null) policies
57	        else policies.filter { it.type == type }
58	    }
59	}

1	# LearnMart - Offline Training Commerce & Classroom
2	
3	Android offline-first application for a US-based professional training provider. Supports course commerce, enrollment governance, classroom operations, grading, financial controls, and operational continuity on a single device with no internet dependency.
4	
5	## Build & Test
6	
7	```bash
8	# Docker build (produces APK)
9	docker compose up --build learnmart-build
10	
11	# Docker test (runs unit tests)
12	docker compose up --build learnmart-test
13	
14	# Local Gradle (requires Android SDK)
15	./gradlew assembleDebug --stacktrace
16	./gradlew testDebugUnitTest --stacktrace
17	
18	# Run tests via script
19	./run_tests.sh
20	```
21	
22	## Module Overview
23	
24	| Layer | Directory | Purpose |
25	|-------|-----------|---------|
26	| **Domain Models** | `domain/model/` | Enums, data classes, state machines |
27	| **Use Cases** | `domain/usecase/` | Business logic with permission enforcement |
28	| **Repositories** | `domain/repository/` | Interfaces; `data/repository/` has impls |
29	| **Room Entities/DAOs** | `data/local/entity/`, `data/local/dao/` | Encrypted SQLite persistence |
30	| **Security** | `security/` | Credential hashing, session management, signature verification |
31	| **DI** | `di/` | Hilt modules for database, repositories, SQLDelight |
32	| **UI** | `ui/screens/` | Jetpack Compose screens + ViewModels |
33	| **Workers** | `worker/` | WorkManager jobs for timeouts, reconciliation, and backup |
34	| **Nav Guards** | `ui/navigation/` | Route-level permission guards for operations screens |
35	
36	## Persistence Architecture: Room + SQLDelight
37	
38	- **Room** owns the primary encrypted database (`learnmart_encrypted.db`), all schema creation, migrations, entity management, and transactional writes. It handles heavy relational persistence.
39	- **SQLDelight** (via `SqlDriver`) owns a separate lightweight database (`learnmart_queries.db`) for type-safe audit query reads. The `SqlDelightAuditRepository` mirrors audit events from Room for optimized read access. Room remains the authoritative source of truth.
40	- **SQLCipher** provides AES-256 encryption at rest for the primary Room database.
41	
42	## Key Documentation
43	
44	- [ASSUMPTIONS.md](docs/ASSUMPTIONS.md) - 36 implementation assumptions
45	- [Operator Guide](docs/operator-guide.md) - Admin usage, demo accounts, policies
46	- [Reconciliation Guide](docs/reconciliation-guide.md) - Settlement import/reconciliation
47	- [Backup/Restore Guide](docs/backup-restore-guide.md) - AES-256 encrypted backup workflow
48	
49	## Roles
50	
51	| Role | Key Capabilities |
52	|------|-----------------|
53	| Administrator | Full access, policies, backup/restore, user management |
54	| Registrar | Enrollment approvals, seat control, catalog management |
55	| Instructor | Assignments, grading, class instruction |
56	| Teaching Assistant | Grading within assigned scope |
57	| Learner | Browse, enroll, shop, submit work |
58	| Finance Clerk | Payments, refunds, reconciliation, imports |
59	
60	## High-Churn List Strategy
61	
62	The prompt requires "RecyclerView with DiffUtil embedded where needed to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists." The implementation uses Compose `LazyColumn` with stable `key` parameters on all high-churn surfaces. This is an explicit architectural decision documented here:
63	
64	**Why Compose LazyColumn instead of embedded RecyclerView:**
65	Jetpack Compose's `LazyColumn` with stable keys provides the same diff-based incremental update semantics as RecyclerView+DiffUtil. When `key = { it.id }` is specified, Compose tracks item identity across recompositions and only rebinds/recomposes changed items — identical to DiffUtil's `areItemsTheSame`/`areContentsTheSame` behavior. Embedding a RecyclerView inside Compose via `AndroidView` would add interop overhead, break Compose's layout system, and provide no performance benefit.
66	
67	**High-churn surfaces with stable keys:**
68	- Order lists: `items(orders, key = { it.id })` in `OrderListScreen`
69	- Operations lists: `items(importJobs, key = { it.id })`, `items(discrepancyCases, key = { it.id })`, `items(backupArchives, key = { it.id })`, `items(exportJobs, key = { it.id })` in `OperationsScreen`
70	- Dashboard audit events: `items(events, key = { it.id })` in `DashboardScreen`
71	- Enrollment lists: `items(enrollments, key = { it.id })` in `EnrollmentListScreen`
72	- Discrepancy cases: `items(cases, key = { it.id })` in `ReconciliationScreen`
73	- Backup archives: `items(archives, key = { it.id })` in `BackupRestoreScreen`
74	
75	**Additional performance measures:**
76	- State hoisting via `StateFlow` + `collectAsState()` minimizes recomposition scope
77	- Offset-based pagination at the DAO layer for 1000+ item surfaces
78	- Image downsampling and LRU cache constraints (20MB peak) prevent OOM
79	
80	This satisfies the prompt's performance intent (60fps scrolling, no full-list refresh) using the Compose-native equivalent of DiffUtil, which is the standard approach for Compose-based Android apps.
81	
82	## Backup Encryption Model
83	
84	Backups use **AES-256-GCM** with keys derived via **PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256** (120,000 iterations). The archive header contains only the random salt and IV — **no raw key material is stored in the archive**. The encryption key is derived from an operator-configured passphrase (policy `backup_passphrase`).
85	
86	**Important:** Backup creation **fails closed** when no passphrase is configured — there is no default fallback. The administrator must set the `backup_passphrase` policy before creating backups. This prevents confidentiality compromise from predictable defaults.
87	
88	Export/import uses Android SAF (Storage Access Framework) content URIs for scoped-storage compliance on Android 10+. The `BackupRestoreUseCase` provides `exportBackupToStream()` and `restoreFromStream()` for SAF integration. The `BackupRestoreScreen` exposes real SAF-based "Export to File" (via `CreateDocument`) and "Import & Restore from File" (via `OpenDocument`) buttons that route through content URIs.
89	
90	## WorkManager Execution Model
91	
92	Reconciliation and backup are routed through real WorkManager jobs, not inline use-case calls:
93	
94	- **Reconciliation**: `ReconciliationScreen` enqueues via `WorkScheduler.enqueueReconciliationJob()` which creates a `OneTimeWorkRequest<ReconciliationWorker>` with idle+charging constraints.
95	- **Backup**: `BackupRestoreScreen` enqueues via `WorkScheduler.enqueueBackupJob()` which creates a `OneTimeWorkRequest<BackupWorker>` with idle+charging constraints.
96	- Both workers execute the real use-case logic (not placeholders) and write results atomically.
97	- The UI does not run heavy operations inline — it only enqueues and observes.
98	
99	## Operations Route Guards
100	
101	All operations routes (overview, import, reconciliation, backup/restore) are wrapped with `RequireOperationsAccess`, a composable guard that checks permissions via `CheckPermissionUseCase` before rendering content. Unauthorized users see an access-denied message. This is defense-in-depth on top of the existing use-case permission checks.
102	
103	## Instrumented/Manual Verification Notes
104	
105	These features require a real Android device or emulator and cannot be fully validated via unit tests:
106	- SAF file picker for settlement imports (`OpenDocument` in `ImportScreen`)
107	- SAF file picker for backup export (`CreateDocument` in `BackupRestoreScreen`)
108	- SAF file picker for backup restore-from-file (`OpenDocument` in `BackupRestoreScreen`)
109	- Android Keystore-backed database encryption key management
110	- Real backup/restore with encrypted archive file I/O (PBKDF2 key derivation + AES-GCM)
111	- WorkManager job scheduling with idle/charging constraints (requires real device idle state)
112	- SQLCipher database encryption at rest
113	- `RequireOperationsAccess` composable guard rendering (verified via Compose UI tests or manual nav)
114	- Content URI stream handling for scoped storage reads/writes

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "C:\Users\amana\OneDrive\Desktop\Files\Eaglepoint\April 10\Task-143\repo\README.md", "old_string": "## Operations Route Guards

All operations routes (overview, import, reconciliation, backup/restore) are wrapped with `RequireOperationsAccess`, a composable guard that checks permissions via `CheckPermissionUseCase` before rendering content. Unauthorized users see an access-denied message. This is defense-in-depth on top of the existing use-case permission checks.", "new_string": "## Route-Level Permission Guards

All sensitive routes are wrapped with composable permission guards that check authorization via `CheckPermissionUseCase` before rendering content. Unauthorized users see an access-denied message and a back button. This is defense-in-depth on top of use-case permission checks.

**Guarded route categories:**
- **Admin routes** (user management, user detail, create user): `RequirePermission(USER_MANAGE)`
- **Policy routes** (policy list, policy edit): `RequirePermission(POLICY_MANAGE)`
- **Audit route**: `RequirePermission(AUDIT_VIEW)`
- **Enrollment approval**: `RequirePermission(ENROLLMENT_REVIEW)`
- **Grading**: `RequirePermission(ASSESSMENT_GRADE)`
- **Payment recording**: `RequirePermission(PAYMENT_RECORD)`
- **Refund issuance**: `RequirePermission(REFUND_ISSUE)`
- **Operations routes** (overview, import, reconciliation, backup/restore): `RequireOperationsAccess` with role-appropriate permissions

The `RequirePermission` and `RequireOperationsAccess` composables share a common `RouteGuardContent` implementation backed by `OperationsRouteGuardViewModel`."}

- **Audit route**: `RequirePermission(AUDIT_VIEW)`
- **Enrollment approval**: `RequirePermission(ENROLLMENT_REVIEW)`
- **Grading**: `RequirePermission(ASSESSMENT_GRADE)`
- **Payment recording**: `RequirePermission(PAYMENT_RECORD)`

- **Operations routes** (overview, import, reconciliation, backup/restore): `RequireOperationsAccess` with role-appropriate permissions

The `RequirePermission` and `RequireOperationsAccess` composables share a common `RouteGuardContent` implementation backed by `OperationsRouteGuardViewModel`."}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\amana\OneDrive\Desktop\Files\Eaglepoint\April 10\Task-143\repo\app\src\test\java\com\learnmart\app\domain\usecase\commerce\FinancialTransactionAtomicityTest.kt", "content": "package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.commerce

import com.learnmart.app.data.local.LearnMartRoomDatabase
import com.learnmart.app.data.local.dao.BlacklistDao
import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.*
import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
import com.google.common.truth.Truth.assertThat
import io.mockk.*
import kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest
import org.junit.Before
import org.junit.Test
import java.math.BigDecimal
import java.time.Instant

class FinancialTransactionAtomicityTest {
    private lateinit var cartRepository: CartRepository
    private lateinit var orderRepository: OrderRepository
    private lateinit var inventoryRepository: InventoryRepository
    private lateinit var paymentRepository: PaymentRepository
    private lateinit var policyRepository: PolicyRepository
    private lateinit var auditRepository: AuditRepository
    private lateinit var pricingEngine: PricingEngine
    private lateinit var blacklistDao: BlacklistDao
    private lateinit var checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase
    private lateinit var sessionManager: SessionManager
    private lateinit var database: LearnMartRoomDatabase

    private lateinit var checkoutUseCase: CheckoutUseCase
    private lateinit var recordPaymentUseCase: RecordPaymentUseCase
    private lateinit var issueRefundUseCase: IssueRefundUseCase

    private val testCart = Cart(
        id = "cart-1", userId = "user-1", status = CartStatus.ACTIVE,
        checkoutPolicy = CheckoutPolicy.CROSS_CLASS_ALLOWED,
        createdAt = Instant.now(), updatedAt = Instant.now()
    )
    private val testLineItem = CartLineItem(
        id = "item-1", cartId = "cart-1", itemType = LineItemType.COURSE_FEE,
        referenceId = "course-1", classOfferingId = "class-1", title = "Test Course",
        quantity = 1, unitPrice = BigDecimal("50.00"), lineTotal = BigDecimal("50.00"),
        createdAt = Instant.now()
    )

    @Before
    fun setUp() {
        cartRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        orderRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        inventoryRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        paymentRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        policyRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        auditRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        pricingEngine = mockk(relaxed = true)
        blacklistDao = mockk(relaxed = true)
        checkPermission = mockk()
        sessionManager = mockk()
        database = mockk(relaxed = true)

        every { sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() } returns "user-1"
        every { sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId() } returns "session-1"
        coEvery { blacklistDao.isBlacklisted(any()) } returns 0

        checkoutUseCase = CheckoutUseCase(
            cartRepository, orderRepository, inventoryRepository,
            policyRepository, auditRepository, pricingEngine,
            blacklistDao, sessionManager, database
        )
        recordPaymentUseCase = RecordPaymentUseCase(
            paymentRepository, orderRepository, auditRepository,
            checkPermission, sessionManager, database
        )
        issueRefundUseCase = IssueRefundUseCase(
            paymentRepository, orderRepository, policyRepository,
            auditRepository, checkPermission, sessionManager, database
        )
    }

    @Test
    fun `checkout rolls back on transaction failure`() = runTest {
        coEvery { orderRepository.getIdempotencyToken(any()) } returns null
        coEvery { cartRepository.getCartById("cart-1") } returns testCart
        coEvery { cartRepository.getLineItemsForCart("cart-1") } returns listOf(testLineItem)
        coEvery { pricingEngine.getCheckoutPolicy() } returns CheckoutPolicy.CROSS_CLASS_ALLOWED
        coEvery { pricingEngine.calculatePricing(any()) } returns PricingResult(
            subtotal = BigDecimal("50.00"), discountTotal = BigDecimal.ZERO,
            taxAmount = BigDecimal("4.00"), taxRate = BigDecimal("0.08"),
            serviceFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), serviceFeeRate = BigDecimal("0.03"),
            packagingFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), grandTotal = BigDecimal("57.00")
        )
        coEvery { pricingEngine.getMinimumOrderTotal() } returns BigDecimal("25.00")

        // Simulate transaction failure
        coEvery { database.withTransaction(any<suspend () -> Unit>()) } throws RuntimeException("DB write failed")

        val result = checkoutUseCase.submitOrder("cart-1", "token-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.SystemError::class.java)
        val error = result as AppResult.SystemError
        assertThat(error.code).isEqualTo("CHECKOUT_FAILED")
    }

    @Test
    fun `payment recording rolls back on transaction failure`() = runTest {
        coEvery { checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.PAYMENT_RECORD) } returns true
        coEvery { orderRepository.getOrderById("order-1") } returns Order(
            id = "order-1", userId = "user-1", status = OrderStatus.PLACED_UNPAID,
            idempotencyToken = null, subtotal = BigDecimal("50.00"),
            discountTotal = BigDecimal.ZERO, taxAmount = BigDecimal("4.00"),
            serviceFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), packagingFee = BigDecimal("1.50"),
            grandTotal = BigDecimal("57.00"), paidAmount = BigDecimal.ZERO,
            placedAt = Instant.now(), cancelledAt = null, cancelReason = null,
            createdAt = Instant.now(), updatedAt = Instant.now(), version = 1
        )

        coEvery { database.withTransaction(any<suspend () -> Unit>()) } throws RuntimeException("DB write failed")

        val result = recordPaymentUseCase(RecordPaymentRequest(
            orderId = "order-1", amount = BigDecimal("57.00"),
            tenderType = TenderType.CASH, externalReference = null, notes = null
        ))
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.SystemError::class.java)
        val error = result as AppResult.SystemError
        assertThat(error.code).isEqualTo("PAYMENT_RECORDING_FAILED")
    }

    @Test
    fun `refund rolls back on transaction failure`() = runTest {
        coEvery { checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.REFUND_ISSUE) } returns true
        coEvery { orderRepository.getOrderById("order-1") } returns Order(
            id = "order-1", userId = "user-1", status = OrderStatus.PAID,
            idempotencyToken = null, subtotal = BigDecimal("50.00"),
            discountTotal = BigDecimal.ZERO, taxAmount = BigDecimal("4.00"),
            serviceFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), packagingFee = BigDecimal("1.50"),
            grandTotal = BigDecimal("57.00"), paidAmount = BigDecimal("57.00"),
            placedAt = Instant.now(), cancelledAt = null, cancelReason = null,
            createdAt = Instant.now(), updatedAt = Instant.now(), version = 1
        )
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentById("pay-1") } returns PaymentRecord(
            id = "pay-1", orderId = "order-1", amount = BigDecimal("57.00"),
            tenderType = TenderType.CASH, status = PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED,
            externalReference = null, receivedBy = "clerk-1",
            receivedAt = Instant.now(), notes = null, createdAt = Instant.now(), version = 1
        )
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getRefundsForOrder("order-1") } returns emptyList()
        coEvery { policyRepository.getPolicyIntValue(PolicyType.RISK, "max_refunds_per_learner_per_day", 3) } returns 3
        coEvery { paymentRepository.countRefundsForLearnerToday(any(), any(), any()) } returns 0

        coEvery { database.withTransaction(any<suspend () -> Unit>()) } throws RuntimeException("DB write failed")

        val result = issueRefundUseCase(IssueRefundRequest(
            orderId = "order-1", paymentId = "pay-1",
            amount = BigDecimal("10.00"), reason = "Returned item"
        ))
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.SystemError::class.java)
        val error = result as AppResult.SystemError
        assertThat(error.code).isEqualTo("REFUND_FAILED")
    }

    @Test
    fun `checkout succeeds in transaction`() = runTest {
        coEvery { orderRepository.getIdempotencyToken(any()) } returns null
        coEvery { cartRepository.getCartById("cart-1") } returns testCart
        coEvery { cartRepository.getLineItemsForCart("cart-1") } returns listOf(testLineItem)
        coEvery { pricingEngine.getCheckoutPolicy() } returns CheckoutPolicy.CROSS_CLASS_ALLOWED
        coEvery { pricingEngine.calculatePricing(any()) } returns PricingResult(
            subtotal = BigDecimal("50.00"), discountTotal = BigDecimal.ZERO,
            taxAmount = BigDecimal("4.00"), taxRate = BigDecimal("0.08"),
            serviceFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), serviceFeeRate = BigDecimal("0.03"),
            packagingFee = BigDecimal("1.50"), grandTotal = BigDecimal("57.00")
        )
        coEvery { pricingEngine.getMinimumOrderTotal() } returns BigDecimal("25.00")
        coEvery { policyRepository.getPolicyLongValue(any(), any(), any()) } returns 5L

        // Execute the block normally
        coEvery { database.withTransaction(any<suspend () -> Unit>()) } coAnswers {
            firstArg<suspend () -> Unit>().invoke()
        }

        val result = checkoutUseCase.submitOrder("cart-1", "token-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.Success::class.java)
    }
}
"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\amana\OneDrive\Desktop\Files\Eaglepoint\April 10\Task-143\repo\app\src\test\java\com\learnmart\app\domain\usecase\operations\SettlementPaymentUpdateTest.kt", "content": "package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.operations

import com.learnmart.app.data.local.LearnMartRoomDatabase
import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OperationsRepository
import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
import com.google.common.truth.Truth.assertThat
import io.mockk.*
import kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest
import org.junit.Before
import org.junit.Test
import java.math.BigDecimal
import java.time.Instant

class SettlementPaymentUpdateTest {
    private lateinit var operationsRepository: OperationsRepository
    private lateinit var paymentRepository: PaymentRepository
    private lateinit var auditRepository: AuditRepository
    private lateinit var checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase
    private lateinit var sessionManager: SessionManager
    private lateinit var database: LearnMartRoomDatabase
    private lateinit var useCase: ReconciliationUseCase

    @Before
    fun setUp() {
        operationsRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        paymentRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        auditRepository = mockk(relaxed = true)
        checkPermission = mockk()
        sessionManager = mockk()
        database = mockk(relaxed = true)
        every { sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() } returns "finance-1"
        every { sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId() } returns "session-1"
        coEvery { checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.PAYMENT_RECONCILE) } returns true
        coEvery { database.withTransaction(any<suspend () -> Unit>()) } coAnswers {
            firstArg<suspend () -> Unit>().invoke()
        }
        useCase = ReconciliationUseCase(
            operationsRepository, paymentRepository, auditRepository,
            checkPermission, sessionManager, database
        )
    }

    private fun makeRow(externalId: String, paymentRef: String, status: String) = SettlementImportRow(
        id = "row-$externalId", batchId = "batch-1", externalRowId = externalId,
        paymentReference = paymentRef, amount = BigDecimal("100.00"),
        tenderType = "CASH", status = status, transactionDate = Instant.now(),
        rawData = "{}", isValid = true, validationErrors = null, isDuplicate = false
    )

    private fun makePayment(id: String, status: PaymentStatus) = PaymentRecord(
        id = id, orderId = "order-1", amount = BigDecimal("100.00"),
        tenderType = TenderType.CASH, status = status,
        externalReference = null, receivedBy = "clerk", receivedAt = Instant.now(),
        notes = null, createdAt = Instant.now(), version = 1
    )

    @Test
    fun `settlement row with CLEARED status updates payment to CLEARED`() = runTest {
        val row = makeRow("ext-1", "pay-1", "CLEARED")
        val payment = makePayment("pay-1", PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED)

        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById("batch-1") } returns
            SettlementImportBatch("batch-1", "job-1", "BATCH-001", true, 1, Instant.now())
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch("batch-1") } returns listOf(row)
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentById("pay-1") } returns payment
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentsForOrder(any()) } returns emptyList()
        coEvery { operationsRepository.hasAppliedSettlementUpdate(any()) } returns false
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getImportJobById(any()) } returns null

        val result = useCase.runReconciliation("batch-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.Success::class.java)

        // Verify payment status was updated
        coVerify { paymentRepository.updatePaymentStatus("pay-1", PaymentStatus.CLEARED, 1) }
        // Verify dedup record saved
        coVerify { operationsRepository.saveAppliedSettlementUpdate(any(), "row-ext-1", "pay-1", any()) }
    }

    @Test
    fun `re-importing same settlement row does not double-apply payment update`() = runTest {
        val row = makeRow("ext-1", "pay-1", "CLEARED")
        val payment = makePayment("pay-1", PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED)

        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById("batch-1") } returns
            SettlementImportBatch("batch-1", "job-1", "BATCH-001", true, 1, Instant.now())
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch("batch-1") } returns listOf(row)
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentById("pay-1") } returns payment
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentsForOrder(any()) } returns emptyList()
        // Already applied
        coEvery { operationsRepository.hasAppliedSettlementUpdate(any()) } returns true
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getImportJobById(any()) } returns null

        val result = useCase.runReconciliation("batch-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.Success::class.java)

        // Payment status should NOT be updated again
        coVerify(exactly = 0) { paymentRepository.updatePaymentStatus(any(), any(), any()) }
    }

    @Test
    fun `changed status rows apply exactly once per unique idempotency key`() = runTest {
        val row1 = makeRow("ext-1", "pay-1", "CLEARED")
        val row2 = makeRow("ext-1", "pay-1", "VOIDED") // Same external_id, different status

        val payment = makePayment("pay-1", PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED)

        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById("batch-1") } returns
            SettlementImportBatch("batch-1", "job-1", "BATCH-001", true, 2, Instant.now())
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch("batch-1") } returns listOf(row1, row2)
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentById("pay-1") } returns payment
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentsForOrder(any()) } returns emptyList()
        coEvery { operationsRepository.hasAppliedSettlementUpdate(any()) } returns false
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getImportJobById(any()) } returns null

        val result = useCase.runReconciliation("batch-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.Success::class.java)

        // Two different idempotency keys should result in two saves
        coVerify(exactly = 2) { operationsRepository.saveAppliedSettlementUpdate(any(), any(), any(), any()) }
    }

    @Test
    fun `invalid settlement status does not update payment`() = runTest {
        val row = makeRow("ext-1", "pay-1", "UNKNOWN_STATUS")
        val payment = makePayment("pay-1", PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED)

        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById("batch-1") } returns
            SettlementImportBatch("batch-1", "job-1", "BATCH-001", true, 1, Instant.now())
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch("batch-1") } returns listOf(row)
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentById("pay-1") } returns payment
        coEvery { paymentRepository.getPaymentsForOrder(any()) } returns emptyList()
        coEvery { operationsRepository.hasAppliedSettlementUpdate(any()) } returns false
        coEvery { operationsRepository.getImportJobById(any()) } returns null

        val result = useCase.runReconciliation("batch-1")
        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(AppResult.Success::class.java)

        // No payment status update for unknown status
        coVerify(exactly = 0) { paymentRepository.updatePaymentStatus(any(), any(), any()) }
    }
}
"}

Keystore is used only for the primary database encryption key. PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 derives the AES-256 key from passphrase + random salt. The archive header contains only salt and IV — no raw key material.

## Post-Audit Fix Assumptions (Audit Round 2)

43. **Sensitive read authorization**: All read methods on ManageUserUseCase, ManagePolicyUseCase, ManageEnrollmentUseCase (global pending feeds), and ViewAuditLogUseCase (getRecentEvents, countAll) now enforce permission checks. ViewModels access data exclusively through permission-checked use case paths.

44. **Route-level guards for admin routes**: Admin (USER_MANAGE), policy (POLICY_MANAGE), audit (AUDIT_VIEW), enrollment approval (ENROLLMENT_REVIEW), grading (ASSESSMENT_GRADE), payment recording (PAYMENT_RECORD), and refund (REFUND_ISSUE) routes are all wrapped with `RequirePermission` composable guards. This is defense-in-depth on top of use-case permission checks.

45. **Financial transaction atomicity**: CheckoutUseCase, RecordPaymentUseCase, and IssueRefundUseCase wrap all multi-write operations (order creation, line items, price components, payment records, allocations, ledger entries, status updates, audit events) in `database.withTransaction {}` blocks. Failures cause full rollback with no partial state.

46. **Settlement import idempotent payment-status updates**: When reconciliation matches a settlement row to a payment, it maps the settlement status (CLEARED, VOIDED, etc.) to a PaymentStatus and applies it. Idempotency is enforced via a SHA-256 hash of (externalRowId + status) stored in the `settlement_payment_updates` table. Re-imports skip already-applied updates.

47. **Assessment finalization correctness**: The `checkAndFinalizeSubmission` method now queries `getQueueItemsForSubmission(submissionId)` instead of `getPendingQueueByClassOffering("")`. When all subjective items are graded, the submission transitions directly to FINALIZED (not stuck at GRADED).

48. **Import parsing robustness**: CSV parsing uses RFC 4180-compliant field parsing with proper handling of quoted commas, escaped quotes, and whitespace trimming. JSON parsing uses a tokenized approach supporting escaped strings and numeric/boolean values. Both formats apply de-duplication by external_id and reject rows with invalid dates.

49. **Image memory controls**: The current UI uses only vector Material Icons. An `ImageMemoryManager` utility is provided with BitmapFactory downsampling and a bounded LRU cache (20MB budget) for when bitmap images are added. Docs have been corrected to reflect this.

50. **Dashboard navigation**: The dashboard now exposes Catalog, Enrollments, Cart, Orders, Assessments, User Management, Policies, Audit Log, and Operations cards with role-based visibility. All entries route through guarded destinations."}

All changes are in place. Here is the complete report:

---

## 1. Summary of Implemented Fixes

**Issue 1 & 9 — Sensitive-read authorization:** Added permission checks to all read methods on `ManageUserUseCase` (getAllActiveUsers, getUserById, getRolesForUser, searchUsers), `ManagePolicyUseCase` (getAllActivePolicies, getPoliciesByType), `ManageEnrollmentUseCase` (getPendingRequests, getRequestById, getRequestsForClass, getRequestsForLearner with owner-check), and `ViewAuditLogUseCase` (getRecentEvents, countAll). Updated all consuming ViewModels (`DashboardViewModel`, `EnrollmentListViewModel`, `UserListViewModel`, `PolicyListViewModel`) to go through the permission-checked use case paths. Dashboard now only shows audit events to users with `AUDIT_VIEW` permission.

**Issue 2 — Route-level authorization:** Added a generic `RequirePermission` composable guard alongside the existing `RequireOperationsAccess`. Applied route guards to: admin user routes (`USER_MANAGE`), policy routes (`POLICY_MANAGE`), audit route (`AUDIT_VIEW`), enrollment approval (`ENROLLMENT_REVIEW`), grading (`ASSESSMENT_GRADE`), payment recording (`PAYMENT_RECORD`), refund issuance (`REFUND_ISSUE`). All share a common `RouteGuardContent` implementation.

**Issue 3 — Financial transaction atomicity:** Wrapped `CheckoutUseCase.submitOrder`, `RecordPaymentUseCase.invoke`, and `IssueRefundUseCase.invoke` in `database.withTransaction {}` blocks. All multi-write flows (order+lines+components+locks+cart+token+audit, payment+allocation+ledger+status+audit, refund+ledger+status+audit) are atomic. On failure, transactions roll back and return `SystemError` with appropriate code.

**Issue 4 — Idempotent payment-status updates from settlement imports:** Extended `ReconciliationUseCase.runReconciliation` to map settlement row statuses (CLEARED→CLEARED, VOIDED→VOIDED, FLAGGED→DISCREPANCY_FLAGGED, etc.) to `PaymentStatus` transitions. Uses SHA-256 hash of `(externalRowId|status)` as idempotency key. Dedup state persisted in new `settlement_payment_updates` table. Re-imports skip already-applied updates.

**Issue 5 — Assessment finalization correctness:** Fixed `checkAndFinalizeSubmission` to use `getQueueItemsForSubmission(submissionId)` instead of the incorrect `getPendingQueueByClassOffering("")`. Submissions now transition directly to `FINALIZED` when all grading is complete (not stuck at `GRADED`). Added audit events and state transition logs for finalization.

**Issue 6 — Import parsing robustness:** Replaced ad-hoc `String.split(",")` CSV parsing with RFC 4180-compliant field parser handling quoted commas, escaped quotes (`""`), and whitespace trimming. Replaced regex-based JSON parser with tokenized object extraction supporting escaped strings and numeric/boolean values. Both formats apply de-duplication by `external_id` and reject rows with invalid dates.

**Issue 7 — Image memory controls:** Confirmed the app uses only vector Material Icons (no bitmap images). Created `ImageMemoryManager` utility with BitmapFactory downsampling and 20MB-bounded LRU cache for future use. Corrected README to accurately reflect current state.

**Issue 8 — Dashboard navigation exposure:** Added dashboard cards for Catalog (all roles), Enrollments (learner/registrar/admin), Cart (learner), Orders (learner/finance/admin), Assessments (learner/instructor/TA/admin) with role-appropriate descriptions. All entries route through guarded destinations.

## 2. File-by-File Changes

**Modified source files (14):**

## 5. Remaining Assumptions or Risks

1. **Room migration v5→v6**: The schema version bump requires either destructive migration or an explicit `Migration(5, 6)` object for production. The current codebase should use `fallbackToDestructiveMigration()` per existing pattern, but for production data preservation a manual migration adding the `settlement_payment_updates` table is recommended.

2. **Compose route guard Hilt scope**: Each `RequirePermission` call creates a new `OperationsRouteGuardViewModel` via `hiltViewModel()`. For nav destinations with arguments, this is correct per-destination behavior. However, if the same destination is revisited rapidly, Hilt's scoping ensures no duplicate checks.

3. **Settlement status mapping**: The mapping from settlement file statuses to `PaymentStatus` is based on common conventions (CLEARED→CLEARED, VOIDED→VOIDED, etc.). Operators may need to configure additional mappings for their specific settlement file formats.

4. **EnrollmentListViewModel pending requests**: The pending requests feed now only loads for users with `ENROLLMENT_REVIEW` permission. Learners see only their own requests/enrollments.

5. **Bitmap image loading**: `ImageMemoryManager` is provided but not actively wired to any screen since no bitmap images are currently used. When catalog images are added, it should be integrated.

46	    private const val PREF_PASSPHRASE_IV = "db_passphrase_iv"
47	    private const val ANDROID_KEYSTORE = "AndroidKeyStore"

87	    /**
88	     * Retrieves or creates a database encryption passphrase backed by Android Keystore.
89	     *
90	     * On first launch a random passphrase is generated, encrypted with an AES key held
91	     * in Android Keystore, and the cipher-text + IV are persisted in SharedPreferences.
92	     * On subsequent launches the cipher-text is decrypted using the same Keystore key.
93	     *
94	     * In environments where the Android Keystore is unavailable (e.g. Docker-based
95	     * test runners, Robolectric), the function falls back to a deterministic derived
96	     * key and logs a warning.
97	     */
98	    private fun getOrCreateDatabaseKey(context: Context): String {
99	        return try {
100	            getOrCreateKeystoreBackedKey(context)
101	        } catch (e: Exception) {
102	            Log.w(
103	                TAG,
104	                "Android Keystore unavailable; falling back to derived key. " +
105	                    "This is expected in test/CI environments. Error: ${e.message}"
106	            )
107	            getFallbackDerivedKey(context)
108	        }
109	    }
110	
111	    private fun getOrCreateKeystoreBackedKey(context: Context): String {
112	        val prefs = context.getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
113	
114	        val existingEncrypted = prefs.getString(PREF_ENCRYPTED_PASSPHRASE, null)
115	        val existingIv = prefs.getString(PREF_PASSPHRASE_IV, null)
116	
117	        if (existingEncrypted != null && existingIv != null) {
118	            // Decrypt the stored passphrase using the Keystore key
119	            val keystoreKey = getKeystoreKey()
120	            val cipher = Cipher.getInstance(AES_GCM_TRANSFORMATION)
121	            val iv = Base64.decode(existingIv, Base64.NO_WRAP)
122	            cipher.init(Cipher.DECRYPT_MODE, keystoreKey, GCMParameterSpec(GCM_TAG_LENGTH, iv))
123	            val decrypted = cipher.doFinal(Base64.decode(existingEncrypted, Base64.NO_WRAP))
124	            return String(decrypted, Charsets.UTF_8)
125	        }
126	
127	        // First launch: generate a random passphrase, encrypt it, and store it
128	        val rawPassphrase = java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString() +
129	            java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString()
130	
131	        val keystoreKey = getOrCreateKeystoreAesKey()
132	        val cipher = Cipher.getInstance(AES_GCM_TRANSFORMATION)
133	        cipher.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, keystoreKey)
134	
135	        val encryptedBytes = cipher.doFinal(rawPassphrase.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8))
136	        val iv = cipher.iv
137	
138	        prefs.edit()
139	            .putString(PREF_ENCRYPTED_PASSPHRASE, Base64.encodeToString(encryptedBytes, Base64.NO_WRAP))
140	            .putString(PREF_PASSPHRASE_IV, Base64.encodeToString(iv, Base64.NO_WRAP))
141	            .apply()
142	
143	        return rawPassphrase
144	    }
145	
146	    private fun getKeystoreKey(): SecretKey {
147	        val keyStore = KeyStore.getInstance(ANDROID_KEYSTORE)
148	        keyStore.load(null)
149	        val entry = keyStore.getEntry(KEYSTORE_ALIAS, null) as? KeyStore.SecretKeyEntry
150	            ?: throw IllegalStateException("Keystore key '$KEYSTORE_ALIAS' not found")
151	        return entry.secretKey
152	    }
153	
154	    private fun getOrCreateKeystoreAesKey(): SecretKey {
155	        val keyStore = KeyStore.getInstance(ANDROID_KEYSTORE)
156	        keyStore.load(null)
157	
158	        if (keyStore.containsAlias(KEYSTORE_ALIAS)) {
159	            val entry = keyStore.getEntry(KEYSTORE_ALIAS, null) as KeyStore.SecretKeyEntry
160	            return entry.secretKey
161	        }
162	
163	        val keyGenerator = KeyGenerator.getInstance(
164	            KeyProperties.KEY_ALGORITHM_AES,
165	            ANDROID_KEYSTORE
166	        )
167	        val spec = KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder(
168	            KEYSTORE_ALIAS,
169	            KeyProperties.PURPOSE_ENCRYPT or KeyProperties.PURPOSE_DECRYPT
170	        )
171	            .setBlockModes(KeyProperties.BLOCK_MODE_GCM)
172	            .setEncryptionPaddings(KeyProperties.ENCRYPTION_PADDING_NONE)
173	            .setKeySize(256)
174	            .build()
175	
176	        keyGenerator.init(spec)
177	        return keyGenerator.generateKey()
178	    }
179	
180	    /**
181	     * Fallback for environments where Android Keystore is not available (e.g. Docker,
182	     * Robolectric). Generates a random key once and stores it directly in
183	     * SharedPreferences. This is NOT secure for production use.
184	     */
185	    private fun getFallbackDerivedKey(context: Context): String {
186	        val prefs = context.getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
187	        val existingKey = prefs.getString("fallback_db_key", null)
188	        if (existingKey != null) return existingKey
189	
190	        val key = java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString() + java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString()
191	        prefs.edit().putString("fallback_db_key", key).apply()
192	        return key
193	    }

18	data class PolicyEditUiState(
19	    val policy: Policy? = null,
20	    val newValue: String = "",
21	    val reason: String = "",
22	    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
23	    val isSaving: Boolean = false,
24	    val errorMessage: String? = null,
25	    val isSaved: Boolean = false
26	)

28	@HiltViewModel
29	class PolicyEditViewModel @Inject constructor(
30	    savedStateHandle: SavedStateHandle,
31	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
32	    private val managePolicyUseCase: ManagePolicyUseCase
33	) : ViewModel() {
34	
35	    private val policyId: String = savedStateHandle["policyId"] ?: ""
36	
37	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(PolicyEditUiState())
38	    val uiState: StateFlow<PolicyEditUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
39	
40	    init {
41	        loadPolicy()
42	    }
43	
44	    private fun loadPolicy() {
45	        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
46	            val policy = policyRepository.getPolicyById(policyId)
47	            _uiState.value = if (policy != null) {
48	                PolicyEditUiState(
49	                    policy = policy,
50	                    newValue = policy.value,
51	                    isLoading = false
52	                )
53	            } else {
54	                PolicyEditUiState(
55	                    isLoading = false,
56	                    errorMessage = "Policy not found"
57	                )
58	            }
59	        }
60	    }
61	
62	    fun onValueChanged(value: String) {
63	        _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(newValue = value, errorMessage = null)
64	    }
65	
66	    fun onReasonChanged(reason: String) {
67	        _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(reason = reason)
68	    }
69	
70	    fun save() {
71	        val state = _uiState.value
72	        val policy = state.policy ?: return
73	
74	        if (state.newValue.isBlank()) {
75	            _uiState.value = state.copy(errorMessage = "Value cannot be empty")
76	            return
77	        }
78	
79	        if (state.newValue == policy.value) {
80	            _uiState.value = state.copy(errorMessage = "No changes made")
81	            return
82	        }
83	
84	        _uiState.value = state.copy(isSaving = true, errorMessage = null)
85	
86	        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
87	            when (val result = managePolicyUseCase.updatePolicy(
88	                policyId = policy.id,
89	                newValue = state.newValue,
90	                reason = state.reason.ifBlank { null }
91	            )) {
92	                is AppResult.Success -> {
93	                    _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(isSaving = false, isSaved = true)
94	                }
95	                is AppResult.PermissionError -> {
96	                    _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
97	                        isSaving = false,
98	                        errorMessage = "Permission denied: requires policy.manage"
99	                    )
100	                }
101	                is AppResult.ValidationError -> {
102	                    _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
103	                        isSaving = false,
104	                        errorMessage = result.globalErrors.firstOrNull()
105	                            ?: result.fieldErrors.values.firstOrNull()
106	                            ?: "Validation error"
107	                    )
108	                }
109	                else -> {
110	                    _uiState.value = _uiState.value.copy(
111	                        isSaving = false,
112	                        errorMessage = "Failed to update policy"
113	                    )
114	                }
115	            }
116	        }
117	    }
118	}

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "C:\Users\amana\OneDrive\Desktop\Files\Eaglepoint\April 10\Task-143\repo\app\src\main\java\com\learnmart\app\di\DatabaseModule.kt", "old_string": "    /**
     * Fallback for environments where Android Keystore is not available (e.g. Docker,
     * Robolectric). Generates a random key once and stores it directly in
     * SharedPreferences. This is NOT secure for production use.
     */
    private fun getFallbackDerivedKey(context: Context): String {
        val prefs = context.getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        val existingKey = prefs.getString("fallback_db_key", null)
        if (existingKey != null) return existingKey

        val key = java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString() + java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString()
        prefs.edit().putString("fallback_db_key", key).apply()
        return key
    }", "new_string": "    /**
     * Fallback for environments where Android Keystore is not available (e.g. Docker,
     * Robolectric). Only reachable in debug/test builds — [getOrCreateDatabaseKey]
     * throws in release builds before reaching this path.
     *
     * Derives a key from the package name + a per-install random salt so it is not
     * trivially predictable, but this is NOT equivalent to Keystore-backed encryption.
     */
    private fun getFallbackDerivedKey(context: Context): String {
        val prefs = context.getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        val existingKey = prefs.getString("fallback_db_key_v2", null)
        if (existingKey != null) return existingKey

        // Derive from package + random salt so different apps/installs diverge
        val salt = java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString()
        val raw = "${context.packageName}:$salt:${java.util.UUID.randomUUID()}"
        val digest = java.security.MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256")
        val key = digest.digest(raw.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8))
            .joinToString("") { "%02x".format(it) }
        prefs.edit().putString("fallback_db_key_v2", key).apply()
        Log.w(TAG, "DEBUG/TEST-ONLY fallback DB key generated. Not for production use.")
        return key
    }"}

Both fixes are complete. Here's the summary:

---

## Fix 1: Authorization model — `ManagePolicyUseCase.getPolicyValue` and `PolicyEditViewModel`

**`ManagePolicyUseCase.kt`** (lines 142-153):
- `getPolicyValue` now returns `AppResult<String>` and enforces `POLICY_MANAGE` permission check before returning the value. Unauthorized callers get `AppResult.PermissionError`.
- Added new `getPolicyById` method that also enforces `POLICY_MANAGE` permission. This is the authorized path for `PolicyEditViewModel` to load a policy for editing.

**`PolicyEditViewModel.kt`** (complete rewrite):
- Removed the `PolicyRepository` constructor parameter entirely — the ViewModel no longer has direct repository access.
- `loadPolicy()` now calls `managePolicyUseCase.getPolicyById(policyId)` instead of `policyRepository.getPolicyById(policyId)`, routing through the permission-checked use case.
- Permission errors during load are shown to the user as `"Permission denied: requires policy.manage"`.

**Note**: Internal system callers (PricingEngine, BackupWorker, SimilarityEngine, ImportSettlementUseCase) already use `policyRepository.getPolicyValue` directly — they do not go through `ManagePolicyUseCase`, so they are unaffected by this permission gate. This is correct: those are internal config lookups, not admin read paths.

**New tests** in `ManagePolicyUseCaseReadAuthTest.kt`:
- `getPolicyValue denied without POLICY_MANAGE permission`
- `getPolicyValue succeeds with POLICY_MANAGE permission`
- `getPolicyById denied without POLICY_MANAGE permission`
- `getPolicyById succeeds with POLICY_MANAGE permission`
- `getPolicyById returns NotFound for missing policy`

## Fix 2: Production keystore fallback insecurity

**`DatabaseModule.kt`** (lines 99-143, 222-234):

`getOrCreateDatabaseKey`:
- Now calls `isTestOrDebugEnvironment(context)` before allowing the fallback path.
- **Release builds** (non-debuggable, no test runner): throws `IllegalStateException` with a clear message — the app refuses to start with an insecure key.
- **Debug/test builds**: fallback is still permitted with a warning log.

`isTestOrDebugEnvironment`:
- New method that checks `ApplicationInfo.FLAG_DEBUGGABLE` (set by Gradle `buildType`).
- Also detects Robolectric and AndroidJUnitRunner test runners via class-name probing.
- Returns `false` for production builds, causing `getOrCreateDatabaseKey` to throw.

`getFallbackDerivedKey`:
- Upgraded from plain UUID to `SHA-256(packageName:randomSalt:randomUUID)` — not trivially predictable.
- Uses new pref key `fallback_db_key_v2` so old insecure plain-UUID keys from prior debug builds are not reused.
- Logs explicit `"DEBUG/TEST-ONLY fallback DB key generated"` warning.

**New tests** in `DatabaseKeystoreSecurityTest.kt`:
- Production must not use insecure fallback (documents the contract)
- Fallback key uses SHA-256 derivation (verifies 64-char hex output)
- Pref key is `v2` to avoid reusing old keys
- Keystore alias is deterministic
- GCM tag length is 128 bits

### Suggested fix

1	package com.learnmart.app.worker
2	
3	import android.content.Context
4	import androidx.hilt.work.HiltWorker
5	import androidx.room.withTransaction
6	import androidx.work.CoroutineWorker
7	import androidx.work.WorkerParameters
8	import com.learnmart.app.data.local.LearnMartRoomDatabase
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
11	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OperationsRepository
12	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
13	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
14	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
15	import dagger.assisted.Assisted
16	import dagger.assisted.AssistedInject
17	
18	/**
19	 * Dedicated worker for reconciliation jobs. Executes real reconciliation logic:
20	 * matches settlement rows against payment records, creates discrepancy cases,
21	 * all within a Room transaction. Scheduled with idle+charging constraints.
22	 */
23	@HiltWorker
24	class ReconciliationWorker @AssistedInject constructor(
25	    @Assisted appContext: Context,
26	    @Assisted workerParams: WorkerParameters,
27	    private val operationsRepository: OperationsRepository,
28	    private val paymentRepository: PaymentRepository,
29	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
30	    private val database: LearnMartRoomDatabase
31	) : CoroutineWorker(appContext, workerParams) {
32	
33	    override suspend fun doWork(): Result {
34	        val batchId = inputData.getString("batchId") ?: return Result.failure()
35	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
36	
37	        return try {
38	            val batch = operationsRepository.getSettlementBatchById(batchId)
39	                ?: return Result.failure()
40	
41	            val rows = operationsRepository.getSettlementRowsByBatch(batchId)
42	            val validRows = rows.filter { it.isValid && !it.isDuplicate }
43	            if (validRows.isEmpty()) return Result.failure()
44	
45	            val runId = IdGenerator.newId()
46	            val matches = mutableListOf<ReconciliationMatch>()
47	            val discrepancies = mutableListOf<DiscrepancyCase>()
48	            var matchedCount = 0
49	            var unmatchedCount = 0
50	            val duplicateCount = rows.count { it.isDuplicate }
51	
52	            for (row in validRows) {
53	                val paymentRef = row.paymentReference
54	                if (paymentRef == null) {
55	                    unmatchedCount++
56	                    discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
57	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
58	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
59	                        discrepancyType = "NO_REFERENCE", description = "No payment reference",
60	                        notes = "Row: ${row.externalRowId}", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
61	                        resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
62	                        createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
63	                    ))
64	                    continue
65	                }
66	
67	                val matchedPayment = paymentRepository.getPaymentById(paymentRef)
68	                if (matchedPayment != null) {
69	                    if (matchedPayment.amount.compareTo(row.amount) != 0) {
70	                        discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
71	                            id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
72	                            settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
73	                            discrepancyType = "AMOUNT_MISMATCH",
74	                            description = "Settlement ${row.amount} != payment ${matchedPayment.amount}",
75	                            notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
76	                            resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
77	                            createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
78	                        ))
79	                    }
80	                    matches.add(ReconciliationMatch(
81	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
82	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = matchedPayment.id,
83	                        matchType = "REFERENCE", matchedAt = now
84	                    ))
85	                    matchedCount++
86	                } else {
87	                    unmatchedCount++
88	                    discrepancies.add(DiscrepancyCase(
89	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(), reconciliationRunId = runId,
90	                        settlementRowId = row.id, paymentRecordId = null,
91	                        discrepancyType = "UNMATCHED", description = "No match for: $paymentRef",
92	                        notes = "", status = DiscrepancyCaseStatus.OPEN,
93	                        resolvedBy = null, resolvedAt = null, resolutionNote = null,
94	                        createdBy = "SYSTEM", createdAt = now, updatedAt = now
95	                    ))
96	                }
97	            }
98	
99	            // Atomic write of all reconciliation results within a Room transaction.
100	            // If any write fails, the entire reconciliation run rolls back — no partial state.
101	            val run = ReconciliationRun(
102	                id = runId, batchId = batchId,
103	                matchedCount = matchedCount, unmatchedCount = unmatchedCount,
104	                duplicateCount = duplicateCount, discrepancyCount = discrepancies.size,
105	                runBy = "SYSTEM", runAt = now, status = "COMPLETED"
106	            )
107	            database.withTransaction {
108	                operationsRepository.createReconciliationRun(run)
109	                if (matches.isNotEmpty()) operationsRepository.createReconciliationMatches(matches)
110	                discrepancies.forEach { operationsRepository.createDiscrepancyCase(it) }
111	
112	                val importJob = operationsRepository.getImportJobById(batch.importJobId)
113	                if (importJob != null) {
114	                    operationsRepository.updateImportJob(importJob.copy(
115	                        status = ImportJobStatus.APPLIED, updatedAt = now
116	                    ))
117	                }
118	
119	                auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
120	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(), actorId = "SYSTEM", actorUsername = "SYSTEM",
121	                    actionType = AuditActionType.RECONCILIATION_RUN,
122	                    targetEntityType = "ReconciliationRun", targetEntityId = runId,
123	                    beforeSummary = null,
124	                    afterSummary = "matched=$matchedCount, unmatched=$unmatchedCount, discrepancies=${discrepancies.size}",
125	                    reason = null, sessionId = null,
126	                    outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS, timestamp = now, metadata = null
127	                ))
128	            }
129	
130	            Result.success()
131	        } catch (e: Exception) {
132	            if (runAttemptCount < 4) Result.retry() else Result.failure()
133	        }
134	    }
135	}

5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.CourseRepository
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.EnrollmentRepository
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PolicyRepository

13	import javax.inject.Inject

15	class SubmitEnrollmentUseCase @Inject constructor(
16	    private val enrollmentRepository: EnrollmentRepository,
17	    private val courseRepository: CourseRepository,
18	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
19	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
20	    private val blacklistDao: BlacklistDao,
21	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
22	) {
23	    suspend operator fun invoke(
24	        classOfferingId: String,
25	        priorityTier: Int = 0
26	    ): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
27	        val learnerId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId()
28	            ?: return AppResult.PermissionError("Not authenticated")
29	
30	        // Validate class offering
31	        val classOffering = courseRepository.getClassOfferingById(classOfferingId)
32	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("CLASS_NOT_FOUND")
33	
34	        if (classOffering.status != ClassOfferingStatus.OPEN) {
35	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
36	                globalErrors = listOf("Class is not open for enrollment (status: ${classOffering.status})")
37	            )
38	        }
39	
40	        // Check for duplicate active request
41	        val activeCount = enrollmentRepository.countActivePendingRequests(learnerId, classOfferingId)
42	        if (activeCount > 0) {
43	            return AppResult.ConflictError("DUPLICATE_REQUEST",
44	                "You already have an active enrollment request for this class")
45	        }
46	
47	        // Check for existing active enrollment
48	        val existing = enrollmentRepository.getActiveEnrollment(learnerId, classOfferingId)
49	        if (existing != null) {
50	            return AppResult.ConflictError("ALREADY_ENROLLED",
51	                "You are already enrolled in this class")
52	        }
53	
54	        // Blacklist check - required before enrollment approval finalization
55	        if (blacklistDao.isBlacklisted(learnerId) > 0) {
56	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
57	                globalErrors = listOf("Enrollment denied: learner is blacklisted")
58	            )
59	        }
60	
61	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
62	        val expiryHours = policyRepository.getPolicyLongValue(
63	            PolicyType.ENROLLMENT, "request_expiry_hours", 48
64	        )
65	        val expiresAt = TimeUtils.hoursFromNow(expiryHours)
66	
67	        // Create eligibility snapshot
68	        val eligibilitySnapshot = evaluateEligibility(learnerId, classOfferingId)
69	
70	        // Create request
71	        val requestId = IdGenerator.newId()
72	        val request = EnrollmentRequest(
73	            id = requestId,
74	            learnerId = learnerId,
75	            classOfferingId = classOfferingId,
76	            status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.SUBMITTED,
77	            priorityTier = priorityTier,
78	            submittedAt = now,
79	            expiresAt = expiresAt,
80	            approvalFlowId = null,
81	            eligibilitySnapshotId = eligibilitySnapshot.id,
82	            createdAt = now,
83	            updatedAt = now,
84	            version = 1
85	        )
86	
87	        enrollmentRepository.createRequest(request)
88	        enrollmentRepository.createEligibilitySnapshot(eligibilitySnapshot)
89	
90	        // Audit
91	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
92	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
93	            actorId = learnerId,
94	            actorUsername = null,
95	            actionType = AuditActionType.ENROLLMENT_REQUESTED,
96	            targetEntityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
97	            targetEntityId = requestId,
98	            beforeSummary = null,
99	            afterSummary = "classOfferingId=$classOfferingId, status=SUBMITTED",
100	            reason = null,
101	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
102	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
103	            timestamp = now,
104	            metadata = null
105	        ))
106	
107	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
108	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
109	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
110	            entityId = requestId,
111	            fromState = "NONE",
112	            toState = "SUBMITTED",
113	            triggeredBy = learnerId,
114	            reason = null,
115	            timestamp = now
116	        ))
117	
118	        // Check capacity and route
119	        val enrolledCount = enrollmentRepository.countActiveEnrollmentsForClass(classOfferingId)
120	        if (enrolledCount >= classOffering.hardCapacity) {
121	            // Over capacity - check waitlist
122	            val waitlistEnabled = classOffering.waitlistEnabled
123	            if (waitlistEnabled) {
124	                return addToWaitlist(request, classOffering)
125	            } else {
126	                return rejectOverCapacity(request)
127	            }
128	        }
129	
130	        // Route to approval flow
131	        return routeToApproval(request)
132	    }
133	
134	    private suspend fun evaluateEligibility(
135	        learnerId: String, classOfferingId: String
136	    ): EnrollmentEligibilitySnapshot {
137	        // Basic eligibility check - extensible for future rules
138	        return EnrollmentEligibilitySnapshot(
139	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
140	            enrollmentRequestId = "", // Set after request creation
141	            learnerId = learnerId,
142	            classOfferingId = classOfferingId,
143	            isEligible = true,
144	            eligibilityFlags = """{"no_blacklist":true,"class_open":true}""",
145	            evaluatedAt = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
146	        )
147	    }
148	
149	    private suspend fun addToWaitlist(
150	        request: EnrollmentRequest,
151	        classOffering: ClassOffering
152	    ): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
153	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
154	        val maxPos = enrollmentRepository.getMaxWaitlistPosition(classOffering.id)
155	
156	        enrollmentRepository.createWaitlistEntry(WaitlistEntry(
157	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
158	            learnerId = request.learnerId,
159	            classOfferingId = classOffering.id,
160	            enrollmentRequestId = request.id,
161	            position = maxPos + 1,
162	            priorityTier = request.priorityTier,
163	            addedAt = now,
164	            offeredAt = null,
165	            offerExpiresAt = null,
166	            status = WaitlistStatus.ACTIVE
167	        ))
168	
169	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(
170	            request.id, EnrollmentRequestStatus.WAITLISTED, request.version
171	        )
172	
173	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
174	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
175	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
176	            entityId = request.id,
177	            fromState = "SUBMITTED",
178	            toState = "WAITLISTED",
179	            triggeredBy = "SYSTEM",
180	            reason = "Class at capacity, added to waitlist",
181	            timestamp = now
182	        ))
183	
184	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
185	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
186	            actorId = request.learnerId,
187	            actorUsername = null,
188	            actionType = AuditActionType.ENROLLMENT_WAITLISTED,
189	            targetEntityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
190	            targetEntityId = request.id,
191	            beforeSummary = "status=SUBMITTED",
192	            afterSummary = "status=WAITLISTED",
193	            reason = "Class at capacity",
194	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
195	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
196	            timestamp = now,
197	            metadata = null
198	        ))
199	
200	        return AppResult.Success(
201	            request.copy(status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.WAITLISTED),
202	            warnings = listOf("Class is at capacity. You have been added to the waitlist.")
203	        )
204	    }
205	
206	    private suspend fun rejectOverCapacity(request: EnrollmentRequest): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
207	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
208	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(
209	            request.id, EnrollmentRequestStatus.REJECTED, request.version
210	        )
211	
212	        enrollmentRepository.createDecisionEvent(EnrollmentDecisionEvent(
213	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
214	            enrollmentRequestId = request.id,
215	            approvalTaskId = null,
216	            decision = "REJECTED",
217	            decidedBy = "SYSTEM",
218	            reason = "Class at capacity and waitlist is disabled",
219	            timestamp = now
220	        ))
221	
222	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
223	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
224	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
225	            entityId = request.id,
226	            fromState = "SUBMITTED",
227	            toState = "REJECTED",
228	            triggeredBy = "SYSTEM",
229	            reason = "Over capacity, no waitlist",
230	            timestamp = now
231	        ))
232	
233	        return AppResult.ValidationError(
234	            globalErrors = listOf("Class is at full capacity and waitlist is not enabled")
235	        )
236	    }
237	
238	    private suspend fun routeToApproval(request: EnrollmentRequest): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
239	        // Find approval flow
240	        val flow = enrollmentRepository.getApprovalFlowsForClass(request.classOfferingId).firstOrNull()
241	            ?: enrollmentRepository.getDefaultApprovalFlow()
242	
243	        if (flow == null) {
244	            // No approval flow defined - auto-approve
245	            return autoApprove(request)
246	        }
247	
248	        val steps = enrollmentRepository.getStepsForFlow(flow.id)
249	        if (steps.isEmpty()) {
250	            return autoApprove(request)
251	        }
252	
253	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
254	        val expiryHours = 48L // Matches request expiry
255	
256	        // Update request with approval flow reference
257	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequest(request.copy(
258	            status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.PENDING_APPROVAL,
259	            approvalFlowId = flow.id,
260	            updatedAt = now,
261	            version = request.version + 1
262	        ))
263	
264	        // Create approval tasks based on flow type
265	        when (flow.flowType) {
266	            ApprovalFlowType.SERIAL -> {
267	                // Only create task for first step
268	                val firstStep = steps.first()
269	                enrollmentRepository.createApprovalTask(EnrollmentApprovalTask(
270	                    id = IdGenerator.newId(),
271	                    enrollmentRequestId = request.id,
272	                    stepDefinitionId = firstStep.id,
273	                    assignedToUserId = null,
274	                    assignedToRoleType = firstStep.approverRoleType,
275	                    status = ApprovalTaskStatus.PENDING,
276	                    decidedBy = null,
277	                    decidedAt = null,
278	                    notes = null,
279	                    createdAt = now,
280	                    expiresAt = TimeUtils.hoursFromNow(expiryHours)
281	                ))
282	            }
283	            ApprovalFlowType.PARALLEL -> {
284	                // Create tasks for all steps
285	                val tasks = steps.map { step ->
286	                    EnrollmentApprovalTask(
287	                        id = IdGenerator.newId(),
288	                        enrollmentRequestId = request.id,
289	                        stepDefinitionId = step.id,
290	                        assignedToUserId = null,
291	                        assignedToRoleType = step.approverRoleType,
292	                        status = ApprovalTaskStatus.PENDING,
293	                        decidedBy = null,
294	                        decidedAt = null,
295	                        notes = null,
296	                        createdAt = now,
297	                        expiresAt = TimeUtils.hoursFromNow(expiryHours)
298	                    )
299	                }
300	                enrollmentRepository.createApprovalTasks(tasks)
301	            }
302	        }
303	
304	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
305	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
306	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
307	            entityId = request.id,
308	            fromState = "SUBMITTED",
309	            toState = "PENDING_APPROVAL",
310	            triggeredBy = "SYSTEM",
311	            reason = "Routed to ${flow.flowType} approval flow: ${flow.name}",
312	            timestamp = now
313	        ))
314	
315	        return AppResult.Success(
316	            request.copy(
317	                status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.PENDING_APPROVAL,
318	                approvalFlowId = flow.id
319	            )
320	        )
321	    }
322	
323	    private suspend fun autoApprove(request: EnrollmentRequest): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
324	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
325	
326	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(

328	        )
329	
330	        enrollmentRepository.createDecisionEvent(EnrollmentDecisionEvent(

334	            decision = "APPROVED",
335	            decidedBy = "SYSTEM",
336	            reason = "Auto-approved: no approval flow configured",
337	            timestamp = now
338	        ))
339	
340	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
341	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
342	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
343	            entityId = request.id,
344	            fromState = "SUBMITTED",
345	            toState = "APPROVED",
346	            triggeredBy = "SYSTEM",
347	            reason = "Auto-approved",
348	            timestamp = now
349	        ))
350	
351	        // Create enrollment record
352	        enrollmentRepository.createEnrollmentRecord(EnrollmentRecord(
353	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
354	            learnerId = request.learnerId,
355	            classOfferingId = request.classOfferingId,
356	            enrollmentRequestId = request.id,
357	            enrolledAt = now,
358	            completedAt = null,
359	            withdrawnAt = null,
360	            status = EnrollmentRecordStatus.ACTIVE
361	        ))
362	
363	        // Update enrolled count
364	        courseRepository.adjustEnrolledCount(request.classOfferingId, 1)
365	
366	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(
367	            request.id, EnrollmentRequestStatus.ENROLLED, request.version + 1
368	        )
369	
370	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
371	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
372	            actorId = request.learnerId,
373	            actorUsername = null,
374	            actionType = AuditActionType.ENROLLMENT_APPROVED,
375	            targetEntityType = "EnrollmentRequest",

377	            beforeSummary = null,
378	            afterSummary = "Auto-enrolled",
379	            reason = null,
380	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
381	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
382	            timestamp = now,
383	            metadata = null
384	        ))
385	
386	        return AppResult.Success(
387	            request.copy(status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.ENROLLED)
388	        )
389	    }
390	}
